source_feedback: Maintainer-curated verdicts for the Sentrux review packet plus seeded self-defect and remediation artifacts. Replay-backed large_file watchpoints stay present, and direct seeded verdicts now cover the under-evidenced rule, obligation, clone, and zero-config signals.

verdicts.engineer_note: This module recurs across multiple replay scenarios and is the clearest concentrated metrics maintenance surface. Keep it ahead of isolated handler helpers when large-file pressure needs triage.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Keep this near the top of the maintainer summary as a replay-backed refactor target, but still frame it as a watchpoint until non-size evidence is stronger.

verdicts.engineer_note: A 3.6k-line handler entry surface is more important than adjacent support-file growth because it indicates boundary aggregation pressure in the MCP server layer.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Surface this as a headline architecture watchpoint and rank it above narrower support-file large-file findings from the same replay family.

verdicts.engineer_note: This file sits on the session-introduced-clone replay path, so it is a better followthrough watchpoint than generic handler support code growth.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Keep this visible as a clone-followthrough watchpoint without overstating it as direct clone evidence.

verdicts.engineer_note: The classification handler is materially larger than nearby formatting helpers and is a more credible contained cleanup target inside the same boundary family.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Keep this in the maintainer packet as secondary cleanup and do not let smaller nearby handler files outrank it.

verdicts.engineer_note: This is the only replay-backed large-file sample directly sitting on the clone-priority scenario surface, so it should stay visible even though the replay still failed to emit explicit clone kinds.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Keep this present as a clone-adjacent watchpoint, but do not promote it to trusted clone evidence until replay or live runs emit the expected clone signal kinds.

verdicts.engineer_note: The seeded self-defect and successful remediation both confirm that this raw-read pattern is actionable direct boundary evidence, not just adjacent structural debt.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Promote this as trusted boundary-discipline evidence when the same projection bypass pattern appears in live or replay runs.

verdicts.engineer_note: This seeded contract-surface miss reproduced cleanly and remediated cleanly, so it should count as direct propagation evidence instead of leaving the scorecard dependent on large-file stand-ins.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Keep this near the top of the maintainer summary as trusted propagation evidence whenever sibling contract surfaces drift.

verdicts.engineer_note: The self-defect shows direct post-baseline duplicate introduction and the remediation clears it cleanly, so this is better clone evidence than replay-only large-file followthrough.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Keep this visible as direct clone followthrough evidence while still framing it as a watchpoint until live sessions clear the same pattern reliably.

verdicts.engineer_note: The seeded committed-clone drift case shows a direct followthrough miss between src/source.ts and src/copy.ts, and the live repair cleared it without regressions. That is stronger clone evidence than clone-adjacent large-file pressure.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Keep this visible as direct clone followthrough evidence when one side of a committed clone pair changes without syncing its sibling, and rank it above generic clone-adjacent structural watchpoints.

verdicts.engineer_note: This seeded deep-import violation gives Sentrux a concrete zero-config boundary sample with both detection and remediation evidence, which was previously missing from the maintained review set.
verdicts.expected_v2_behavior: Keep this as a maintained watchpoint and use it to validate zero-config boundary ranking against large-file crowd-out.
